reviewSummary.summary: The draft captures the primary guest-state navigation controls required by the workflow, but it includes noisy generic elements, inconsistent naming, and weak locators that reduce downstream automation reliability and page model clarity.

issues.message: The locator xpath=//a is overly generic and likely to match multiple links across the SPA, making downstream automation brittle and ambiguous.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the generic link element entirely because the Home button already represents the intended navigation control with a stable ID locator.

issues.message: The background content container is a layout-only element and does not provide meaningful workflow interaction or validation value.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the background_content element from the approved page model unless a future workflow explicitly requires content-area visibility validation.

issues.message: The element name lacks readability and business clarity because the words are concatenated.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to customer_signup_button for consistent semantic naming.

issues.message: The element name lacks readability and business clarity because the words are concatenated.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to clean_survey_button for consistent semantic naming.

issues.message: The element name lacks readability and business clarity because the words are concatenated.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to site_survey_button for consistent semantic naming.

issues.message: The workflow consistently refers to this control as the Person/Profile button, but the approved name loses that contextual meaning.
issues.suggested_fix: Rename the element to person_profile_button to align with workflow terminology and improve downstream keyword readability.

issues.message: The underlying shadow DOM native control is an anchor element rather than a pure button, which may affect navigation-oriented keyword generation.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep the semantic business meaning as a navigation button but document or model it as a navigation control if the framework distinguishes navigation actions from button clicks.

issues.message: The workflow requires validation that authenticated-only controls and the logged-in user name button are absent in guest state, but no observable elements or negative-state indicators are modeled to support those assertions.
issues.suggested_fix: Add explicit absent-state verification guidance or identifiable authenticated-state controls if available in future extraction passes.

approved_elements.name: person_profile_button
approved_elements.description: Opens the Login page or Login view from the guest Home state.
